item.title: Bending Spoons defies SaaS slump, surges 40% on first day of trading
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The company has grown rapidly by acquiring and revamping last-generation tech brands like AOL, Eventbrite, Evernote, Meetup, and Vimeo.

item.title: After $18B IPO, Bending Spoons founder says success comes from minimizing luck
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: The co-founders of Bending Spoons, the Italian company quietly buying beloved, ailing Internet brands, learned big lessons from their own startup's failure.

item.title: WhatsApp usernames are already raising impersonation red flags
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Meta says usernames improve privacy, but critics question whether its safeguards can prevent impersonation.

item.title: Apple’s Hide My Email feature has a bug that’s been exposing real email addresses, researcher claims
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Research appears to reveal a bug that could render the feature effectively useless.

item.title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.

item.title: Ashton Kutcher leaving Sound Ventures to launch new VC firm with Morgan Beller
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Sound built its reputation on concentrated, high-conviction bets in category-leading AI labs, while Kutcher's new fund appears to be chasing the layer underneath those companies — the infrastructure and energy that power them.

item.title: Neocloud Together AI raises $800M, leaps to $8.3B valuation
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The AI neocloud provider, which specializes in hosting open source models, last raised at a $3.3 billion valuation in early 2025.

item.title: Lime begins life as a public company after years of uncertainty
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The nine-year-old scooter and bike-share company has said it needs the funds to help pay down around $1 billion in liabilities.

item.title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.

item.title: Even Honda is pivoting to data centers
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Honda wants in on the lucrative energy storage market. This week it began producing batteries destined for data centers, not driveways.

item.title: Autonomous vehicle hype is back, and Humble Robotics is bringing it to freight
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The autonomous vehicle space is starting to feel like a repeat of the 2016 hype cycle. Travis Kalanick is back building a robotics company, and the talent wars and capital are heating up the same way they did the first time around. The money’s flowing back, and it’s the people who lived through that first […]

item.title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.

item.title: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google's 24/7 agentic assistant, Gemini Spark, comes to Mac alongside other improvements, like real-time tracking and support for more apps.

item.title: Sony to end physical PlayStation game disc production in 2028
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Sony will stop producing physical discs for all new PlayStation games beginning in 2028, as the company embraces an all-digital future.

item.title: Builders Stage agenda revealed: Practical strategies for scaling startups at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The Builders Stage is returning to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, bringing together 10,000+ founders, startup operators, and investors for practical conversations. and Q&A on what it takes to build and scale successful companies. Register now to save up to $330.

item.title: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Meta is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business, selling access to AI compute power and models. The move would pit it against the big cloud providers like Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

item.title: The ‘Father of the Internet’ is finally retiring
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

item.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

item.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.

item.title: Startup Battlefield Australia application closes in days: Apply before July 6
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: What if one pitch changed everything? The next company nobody has heard of yet is building something that will matter. It could be yours.
